workflowName: Revise an assignment for current AI conditions
objective: Preserve the learning objective while making permitted AI use, process evidence, and human judgment explicit
trigger: Before the assignment is next published or when institutional AI guidance changes
frequency: Two or more times per term

currentSteps.description: Re-read the learning objectives and current assignment
currentSteps.output: List of objectives and assignment elements that must be preserved
painPoints: The original design rationale is scattered across documents

currentSteps.description: Compare institutional and department AI guidance
painPoints: Guidance is broad, changes over time, and is difficult to translate into one assignment
decisionsRequired: Which guidance applies to this course and task

currentSteps.description: Decide where AI use is allowed, required, or prohibited
painPoints: Rules must be clear without replacing the professor's pedagogical judgment

currentSteps.description: Revise instructions, process evidence, and rubric criteria
painPoints: Changes in one document can contradict another and create student confusion
decisionsRequired: What evidence demonstrates the intended learning

currentSteps.description: Test the revised assignment and approve it for publication
currentSteps.tool: Institution-approved AI tool and learning management system
painPoints: A plausible-looking draft can still weaken the objective or violate tool rules

majorFrustrations: Translating broad policy into assignment-level instructions
Keeping the assignment, rubric, and AI-use boundary aligned
Repeating the review when guidance or tools change
sensitiveInformation: Do not enter student submissions, names, grades, accommodations, confidential research, or restricted course materials into unapproved tools.
successDefinition: A professor can publish and later revisit an aligned assignment package with clear AI boundaries and a recorded human approval
desiredImprovement: A repeatable, policy-aware review process that exposes conflicts before publication and can be measured on a second run
